channel.title: Drupal AI
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal AI.

item.title: #3601381: setup-site: positional config source, multiple named configs in a .drupalaibp/ directory, and git ref (branch/MR/fork) support
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601381 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

The drupalaibp one-line installer (version 2.0.0) gains three related improvements to how a config source is specified. First, the config URL or path can now be passed as a plain positional argument (drupalaibp https://git.drupalcode.org/project/foo or drupalaibp ./team-config) instead of requiring the --config-url or --config-path flag; the installer auto-detects whether it is a URL, local directory, or saved global config name. Second, a single source can now carry multiple installer configs as *.json files inside a .drupalaibp/ directory, with automatic selection when only one exists, an interactive menu when several do, and a --config <name> flag for non-interactive disambiguation. Third, URL sources can target a specific branch, tag, or GitLab MR via an @<ref> suffix (foo@1.2.x, foo@!12), or via the new --ref flag. Breaking change (2.x): the root .drupalaibp.json file is no longer recognized anywhere. All configs must now live as *.json files directly under a .drupalaibp/ directory.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: Breaking (2.x): root .drupalaibp.json removed; configs must now live as *.json files directly under .drupalaibp/; CONFIG_BASEDIR now resolves to <source>/.drupalaibp/ so relative hook and copy_paths paths resolve there
  • New API: New positional source argument on setup-site, --config <name> and --ref <ref> flags, and @<ref> suffix syntax on URL sources and the pull command's repo argument

Technical details

The setup-site command's new optional positional source argument is classified by resolve_config_source (src/lib/config/resolve_config_source.sh). Resolution order: an existing local directory routes to --config-path; a git URL (by :// scheme, SCP syntax user@host:path, .git suffix, or owner/repo shorthand expanded to https://git.drupalcode.org/<owner/repo>) routes to --config-url; otherwise the value is looked up as a saved global config name under ~/.drupalaibp/configs/. Combining the positional form with an explicit --config-* flag is a fatal error.

The root .drupalaibp.json convention is replaced by a .drupalaibp/ directory holding one or more *.json config files. select_config (src/lib/config/select_config.sh) picks which config to load: with exactly one *.json it proceeds automatically; with multiple it requires --config <name> or prompts interactively via select_one; under ASK_UNATTENDED it dies naming the available choices. The selector deliberately ignores the config/ YAML import subdir and hook scripts (.sh files), only matching *.json files directly under .drupalaibp/. CONFIG_BASEDIR now points to <source>/.drupalaibp/ (the dirname of the chosen config file) rather than the source root, so relative paths in hooks[].script, copy_paths, and db_import_path resolve inside .drupalaibp/; a "../" prefix reaches the repo root.

Git ref support is added via parse_git_ref (src/lib/config/parse_git_ref.sh), which splits an @<ref> suffix off a source string conservatively: plain SCP URLs (user@host:path with no second @) are left whole; for scheme URLs the split fires only when the remaining source still contains a path after ://. The extracted ref is consumed by _git_clone_ref (src/lib/config/_git_clone_ref.sh): a plain branch or tag name uses git clone --depth 1 -b <ref>; a refs/… refspec or !<iid> (expanded to refs/merge-requests/<iid>/head for GitLab MRs) does a full clone followed by a shallow fetch and checkout FETCH_HEAD. The --ref flag is an alternative to the @<ref> suffix and is accepted on both the positional source form and --config-url. The pull command's repo argument also accepts @<ref>.

_migrate_legacy_configs (src/lib/config/_migrate_legacy_configs.sh) performs a one-time best-effort migration of pre-2.x flat saved configs (~/.drupalaibp/configs/<name>.json) into the nested layout (configs/<name>/.drupalaibp/<name>.json) on load. It is non-fatal: a warning is emitted and the old file is left in place on any failure.

Upgrade

# Migrate a root .drupalaibp.json to the 2.x layout:
mkdir -p .drupalaibp
mv .drupalaibp.json .drupalaibp/config.json
# A lone *.json in .drupalaibp/ is picked automatically — no rename required.

# Relative hook and copy_paths paths now resolve inside .drupalaibp/, not the repo root.
# If hooks[].script was "agent-extras.sh" (resolved from root), move the file:
mv agent-extras.sh .drupalaibp/agent-extras.sh
# Or keep it at the root and update the path to "../agent-extras.sh".

# Before (pre-2.x):
drupalaibp --config-url https://git.drupalcode.org/project/foo

# After (2.x) — positional form:
drupalaibp https://git.drupalcode.org/project/foo

# With a branch ref:
drupalaibp https://git.drupalcode.org/project/foo@1.2.x

# With a GitLab MR ref:
drupalaibp https://git.drupalcode.org/project/foo@!12

# With multiple configs in .drupalaibp/ — pick by name:
drupalaibp https://git.drupalcode.org/project/foo --config config2 --yolo

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 21, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601384: Add an opt-in glab (GitLab CLI) extra: install and authenticate to git.drupalcode.org in the web container
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601384 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer project gains a new opt-in extra called glab that installs the GitLab CLI (pinned at v1.64.0) into the DDEV web container and authenticates it to git.drupalcode.org with a Personal Access Token you paste during setup. Once selected, the tool prompts you to create an api-scoped PAT at drupal.org, stores it in .ddev/.env (chmod 600, gitignored), bakes glab into the web image so it survives rebuilds, and writes a DDEV post-start hook that re-authenticates on every container start. After a single ddev restart, glab mr list, HTTPS git clone, and git push to git.drupalcode.org all work from inside the container. Because the extra requires an interactive token paste, it is never silently installed by --yolo or unattended config-driven runs.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New glab installer extra: select it during drupalaibp setup to install the GitLab CLI (glab v1.64.0) in the DDEV web container and authenticate it to git.drupalcode.org with a PAT.

Technical details

The extra lives in src/lib/extras/extra_glab.sh and follows the project's extra_<id>_meta + extra_<id> function-pair convention, so it is auto-discovered by _extras_discover and appears in the installer's "Any extra Drupal installations?" multi-select without any other file needing to change.

Several sharp edges are addressed by design. The token is stored as DRUPALCODE_GITLAB_TOKEN rather than GITLAB_TOKEN: glab treats GITLAB_TOKEN as a global override applied to every host, which causes 401s against gitlab.com and makes glab auth status exit non-zero even when drupalcode auth works. Feeding the token through glab auth login --stdin instead writes it into glab's own per-host config, scoped to git.drupalcode.org alone. The extra also strips any pre-existing GITLAB_TOKEN or DRUPALCODE_GITLAB_TOKEN lines from .ddev/.env on re-run to undo contamination from earlier versions.

The DDEV post-start hook in .ddev/config.glab.yaml additionally runs git config --global 'credential.https://git.drupalcode.org.helper' '!glab auth git-credential', because glab auth login --stdin does not wire the credential helper automatically (only the interactive prompt does). DDEV merges post-start hooks across config.*.yaml files, so the hook coexists cleanly with the coding-agent add-ons' own post-start hooks.

Verification uses ddev exec glab api user rather than glab auth status, because glab auth status always probes gitlab.com and reports failure even when git.drupalcode.org auth is fully functional. The extra's meta line has no |default suffix, so it is never pre-ticked or silently installed by --yolo. _glab_read_token is factored out into its own function so the bats suite can stub it instead of feeding keystrokes, following the project's "prompts are functions" testing rule.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 21, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3584903: Migrate the inner workings of Surge into AI Best Practices
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 35 files, +5191/−1 · php +5154 · md +31 · template +6/−1
  • Discussion: #3584903 · 6 contributors · 23 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Installing drupal/ai_best_practices now automatically sets up your Drupal project's AI agent context. The Composer plugin creates AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, and ai_best_practices.yaml at your project root, discovers Agent Skills from installed Drupal modules and Composer packages, and copies them into .agents/skills/. Two commands are available after install: composer drupal-ai skills-sync refreshes skills and composer drupal-ai skills-list shows what would be installed. Running composer remove drupal/ai_best_practices reverses the composer.json changes and removes ai_best_practices.yaml. This migrates the orchestration engine from the now-sunsetted Surge project directly into this module so feedback and iteration can happen in one place.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New Composer plugin providing composer drupal-ai skills-sync and composer drupal-ai skills-list commands; new AgentsMdSync, SkillsScanner, SkillsAggregator, SkillValidator, PathSecurity, and ProjectRootResolver classes in the Drupal\AiBestPractices namespace.

Technical details

The MR adds a Composer plugin (AiBestPracticesPlugin) implementing PluginInterface and EventSubscriberInterface. To avoid running before autoload is dumped, it uses a two-phase deferred-sync pattern: ComposerProjectSynchronizer::markSyncPendingFromPackageEvent() writes a marker at vendor/.ai_bp_sync_pending during POST_PACKAGE_INSTALL / POST_PACKAGE_UPDATE, and runDeferredSyncIfPending() acts on it in the later post-install-cmd / post-update-cmd phase. The sync is skipped entirely when the AI_BEST_PRACTICES_SKIP_PACKAGE_SYNC or AI_BEST_PRACTICES_SKIP_COMPOSER_MUTATION environment variables are set.

AgentsMdSync manages a region in AGENTS.md bounded by <!-- ai-best-practices:start --> and <!-- ai-best-practices:end --> HTML comment markers. If the file does not yet exist, the full template from .agents/AGENTS.md.template is written; if it exists without markers, the managed block is prepended; if it exists with markers, only the inner region is replaced (idempotent: returns null and skips the write when the content is unchanged). AgentRedirectFiles copies CLAUDE.md and GEMINI.md from the package's .agents/ directory to the project root only when those files are absent, printing a Composer warning if they already exist.

The skills subsystem (SkillsScanner, SkillsAggregator, SkillValidator) discovers skills from three priority tiers: project-level (.agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, skills/), Drupal modules under modules/* or web/modules/*, and Composer packages under vendor/*. Each skill directory is validated for a SKILL.md file with valid YAML frontmatter per the Agent Skills spec before being copied to the configured output directory (default .agents/skills/). The SkillsListCommand supports both table and xml output formats; the XML path is consumed by file-based AI agents that cannot call tools directly.

On composer remove, ComposerConsumerRollback::stripPluginFromComposerData() removes the drupal-ai script key (and the legacy abp key), the package's config.allow-plugins entry, and prunes now-empty scripts/config objects from composer.json; ai_best_practices.yaml is deleted separately. A known open issue noted during review: module-contributed skills are correctly copied to .agents/skills/ but are not listed in the AGENTS.md managed region, so agents reading that file will not discover them.

Upgrade

No existing code needs updating; this is all new. To add the plugin to an existing project:

composer config allow-plugins.drupal/ai_best_practices true
composer require --dev drupal/ai_best_practices

After install, these files appear at your project root and are safe to commit:

.agents/
AGENTS.md
CLAUDE.md
GEMINI.md
ai_best_practices.yaml

The drupal-ai script is also registered in your composer.json automatically. To refresh skills after adding or updating modules:

composer drupal-ai skills-sync

To remove everything the plugin added:

composer remove drupal/ai_best_practices

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 15, 2026

Key contributors

  • webchick
  • ronaldtebrake (Open Social)
  • trebormc (Menetray)
  • zorz (Point Blank)

Collaboration

webchick tested MR 17 against a fresh Drupal CMS install and filed detailed feedback: the original Composer script name abp was opaque, the presence of environment templates for ddev, lando, and docker-compose pushed the project toward multi-environment support before the team had decided on a single recommended environment, and the install instructions in README assumed an existing site rather than a new one. ronaldtebrake renamed the script to drupal-ai, stripped the multi-environment AGENTS.md template generation from the MR to keep the scope focused, and opened a follow-up issue for the new-site install flow. Separately, zorz tested on a fresh DDEV install and identified a concrete bug: module-contributed skills are correctly copied to .agents/skills/ but are not enumerated in the AGENTS.md managed region, meaning agents reading that file would not learn a module had contributed a skill. ronaldtebrake acknowledged it as a bug to address as a follow-up after the MR lands.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582953: Document how to run evals in various scenarios
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +633/−0 · py +491 · md +140 · gitignore +2
  • Discussion: #3582953 · 3 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

The ai_best_practices project now ships a new evals/compare.py script and expanded CONTRIBUTING.md documentation that let contributors measure whether adding or editing a guidance skill actually changes Claude's output in the desired direction. Before this, there was no automated way to distinguish a skill that genuinely improves model behavior from one that merely adds text. Contributors submitting new or modified skills are now expected to include comparison output in their merge request description.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New evals/compare.py script for A/B comparison of skill effectiveness, with --no-baseline, --baseline, --runs, --model, --output-dir, and --dry-run flags.

Technical details

The new evals/compare.py supports two modes, controlled by flags. The default mode calls git show HEAD:skills/... to retrieve the last committed skill version as a baseline and compares it against the working copy; --baseline /path/to/old.md allows an explicit file instead. The --no-baseline flag substitutes a no-skill prompt for the baseline, measuring whether the skill adds value at all over the raw model.

Both configurations call claude -p with identical flags (--no-session-persistence, --permission-mode auto, --output-format json). The only variable between configs is whether skill content is prepended to the prompt. Grading is fully deterministic: must_contain_any and must_not_contain string checks on the lowercased response, plus optional php -l lint on extracted PHP code blocks via check_php_lint. There is no LLM judge. For isolation, ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md is moved aside during the run and restored afterward so user-level plugins and configuration do not contaminate either config (--no-isolate skips this). Pass rates, token counts, and cost are aggregated across runs and saved as comparison-{timestamp}.json in the skill's eval directory; these files are excluded from git via a new .gitignore.

CONTRIBUTING.md gains a detailed A/B comparison section covering both editing and new-skill workflows, how to read the per-case delta table, and a six-step walkthrough for writing an evals.json from scratch (directory layout, static-checks.json, behavioral case structure, README.md, and verification commands). evals/README.md also gets a short A/B synopsis linking to the full guide.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 3, 2026

Key contributors

  • zorz (Point Blank)

Collaboration

webchick opened the issue and shortly after unassigned herself, crediting zorz for the implementation approach. zorz opened MR !2 with the complete compare.py and documentation. After reviewing, webchick spun off several follow-on issues for real-world eval coverage (#3583192, #3583191) and provider-agnostic runner exploration (#3583202, #3583203), then merged the MR as a foundation to build on. alex ua contributed a substantive Scenario 3 workflow directly in the comments, detailing how a live expert correction becomes an evals.json regression case via must_contain_any and must_not_contain entries. zorz confirmed this mapped cleanly to compare.py's existing design and planned to wire it in after the merge.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3581836: Figure out "information architecture" for this project
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3581836 · 4 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The ai_best_practices project (a Composer plugin that copies AI guidance files into Drupal projects) established its canonical information architecture for skill files. Skills live under skills/, each in its own subdirectory named with a drupal- prefix in kebab-case (for example skills/drupal-automated-testing/), with a SKILL.md file as the required entry point. Every skill must have a matching evals/<skill-name>/ directory containing static structural checks and behavioral test cases. The AGENTS.md index that consumer projects receive is defined in .agents/AGENTS.md.template and merged into project roots by the Composer plugin. These conventions govern all current and future skill contributions to the project.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The architecture aligns with the Agent Skills specification. Each SKILL.md requires only name (must match the directory name) and description frontmatter; the proposal to add Drupal-specific keys such as status, drupal-version, and last-reviewed was explicitly rejected. The description field serves the "when to use" cue that agents use to decide whether to load a skill for a given task.

Skill directory naming follows the pattern <domain>-<task-or-topic> in kebab-case. All skills in this repo carry the drupal- domain prefix to avoid collision with skills from other packages. The gerund-first naming convention originally proposed was relaxed: the final rule is plain-English kebab-case, not a required gerund prefix.

Each skill's evals/<name>/ directory contains static-checks.json (structural assertions run in CI with no API calls, using check types file_exists, agent_skill_frontmatter, contains, order, max_lines), evals.json (behavioral prompt/assertion pairs that require a live AI CLI), and README.md. The compare.py script supports A/B testing of skill edits across multiple AI providers. Skills are auto-discovered from the skills/ directory tree; no manual registration in .agents/AGENTS.md.template is required for the skill to ship to consumers, though an entry there is still recommended so agents know when to load it.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 27, 2026

Collaboration

ronaldtebrake caught that the initial proposal used index.md as the skill entry point instead of the spec-compliant SKILL.md, and restructured the frontmatter to use the Agent Skills specification's required name and description fields, preserving the prose-heavy guidance style while making it interoperable with the broader ecosystem. jrockowitz raised the namespacing concern: without prefixes, skill names from different packages would collide (citing existing conflicts on skills.sh), and pointed to the Drupal.org CLI repo as an example already using namespacing. That observation drove the drupal-* prefix standard that the final architecture adopted.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601380: Separate composer and recipe
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major unknown (0)
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601380 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The recipes key in .drupalaibp.json no longer fetches packages from Composer. It is now apply-only: the installer runs drush recipe for each entry but never calls composer require. Contrib recipes (e.g. drupal/drupal_cms_blog) must be listed in both composer_projects (to fetch) and recipes (to apply). Core or already-present recipes (e.g. core/recipes/standard) still work in recipes alone because no fetch is needed. A new boolean key composer_with_all_dependencies passes --with-all-dependencies (-W) to the composer_projects require step for packages that pull in a broader dependency tree.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: The recipes key is now apply-only; contrib recipes must also be listed in composer_projects for the fetch step. New composer_with_all_dependencies boolean key adds --with-all-dependencies to the composer_projects require call.

Technical details

Previously step_provision_from_config handled the RECIPES array by calling composer require followed by drush recipe in one loop, conflating fetch and apply. The refactor splits them into two strictly ordered phases: COMPOSER_PROJECTS are composer require --no-interaction'd (no apply); RECIPES are drush recipe'd (no fetch). A drupal/<name> entry in RECIPES maps to ../recipes/${name#drupal/} because ddev drush runs from the web/ docroot and Composer installs contrib recipes one level up in recipes/; any other recipe locator (e.g. core/recipes/standard) is passed to drush verbatim. The COMPOSER_WITH_ALL_DEPS global, loaded from the new composer_with_all_dependencies config key, appends --with-all-dependencies to the single composer require call when a recipe or package requires a broader dependency-tree update. The provision_from_config.sh source file and the AGENTS.md, README, and docs/drupalaibp-json.md documentation were all updated to reflect the separation.

Upgrade

Any .drupalaibp.json that listed a contrib recipe solely in recipes must be updated to also list it in composer_projects. Core or built-in recipes that need no download are unaffected.

Before:

{
  "recipes": ["drupal/drupal_cms_blog"]
}

After:

{
  "composer_projects": ["drupal/drupal_cms_blog"],
  "composer_with_all_dependencies": true,
  "recipes": ["drupal/drupal_cms_blog"]
}

composer_with_all_dependencies is optional; set it to true when the recipe pulls in packages that require their shared dependencies to be updated together.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 20, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601379: Config-url clobbers the scaffold composer.json
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601379 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

Two bugs in --config-url provisioning are fixed in this release. First, when building a fresh Drupal scaffold, the config repo's own composer.json was silently overwriting the scaffold's composer.json, wiping the allow-plugins and minimum-stability settings that the installer had just written. This caused subsequent composer require calls to fail or refuse to resolve @dev/@beta packages. The overlay now preserves the scaffold's composer.json and composer.lock. Second, the recipes key in .drupalaibp.json has changed meaning: it now only applies recipes via drush recipe and never fetches them. Contrib recipes must be listed in both composer_projects (to fetch) and recipes (to apply). A new composer_with_all_dependencies config key passes --with-all-dependencies to composer require for packages that need their full dependency tree updated.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

In _copy_config_url_repo (src/lib/config/load_config_url.sh), fresh-scaffold Drupal mode (i.e., IS_DRUPAL=true and no DB_IMPORT) now stashes composer.json and composer.lock before running cp -R "${CONFIG_URL_REPO_DIR}/." . and then restores them afterward. Existing-codebase/non-Drupal mode is unaffected because there the cloned repo is the site root.

In step_provision_from_config (src/lib/steps/provision_from_config.sh), COMPOSER_PROJECTS and RECIPES are now strictly separated: composer_projects calls composer require --no-interaction (optionally with --with-all-dependencies when COMPOSER_WITH_ALL_DEPS=true) and never applies anything; recipes calls ddev drush recipe and never fetches. A drupal/<name> recipe entry is resolved to ../recipes/<name> (the path Composer installs it to), while any other entry (e.g., core/recipes/standard) is passed verbatim to drush as a locator. The new composer_with_all_dependencies key is read into COMPOSER_WITH_ALL_DEPS in load_config.sh and defaults to false.

Upgrade

If your .drupalaibp.json lists contrib recipes (e.g., drupal/my_recipe) under recipes, you must now also list them under composer_projects so they are fetched first:

{
  "composer_projects": ["drupal/my_recipe"],
  "recipes": ["drupal/my_recipe"]
}

Core or already-present recipes (e.g., core/recipes/standard) still only need to appear under recipes.

If any recipe's dependencies conflict with existing constraints, add the composer_with_all_dependencies flag:

{
  "composer_with_all_dependencies": true,
  "composer_projects": ["drupal/my_recipe"],
  "recipes": ["drupal/my_recipe"]
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 20, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3609590: Switch to official MCP PHP SDK Client
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 18 files, +865/−340 · php +852/−331 · txt +7/−2 · md +5/−5 · json +1/−2
  • Diff: a4eccb4
  • Discussion: #3609590 · 3 contributors · 12 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The mcp_client module replaces its underlying transport library, swapping the third-party swisnl/mcp-client for the official modelcontextprotocol/php-sdk (mcp/sdk). The previous library required multiple reflection hacks to work around ReactPHP lifecycle issues that caused "Connection aborted early" errors on Drupal 11.3+. The new SDK is synchronous, built on Symfony HttpClient and Process, and eliminates all of those workarounds. The public interface of MCPClient (its listTools(), executeTool(), and disconnect() methods) is unchanged, so tool plugins work as before. Note that mcp/sdk is still at version 0.6; the module maintainer has announced that only beta releases will be made until the upstream SDK reaches 1.0.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: Drupal\mcp_client\Transporter\CustomHeadersHttpTransporter has been removed; it was public but was an implementation detail of MCPClient.

Technical details

The swisnl/mcp-client: ^0.5 Composer dependency (and the minimum-stability: dev flag it required) is replaced with mcp/sdk: ^0.6. MCPClient is rewritten to use \Mcp\Client, \Mcp\Client\Transport\HttpTransport, and \Mcp\Client\Transport\StdioTransport. The CustomHeadersHttpTransporter class that previously subclassed StreamableHttpTransporter to inject custom HTTP headers and override initializeConnection() to strip problematic register_shutdown_function() calls is deleted entirely. Custom headers now pass through HttpTransport's constructor as a supported first-class argument.

Stderr capture previously relied on \ReflectionClass to read the protected transporter property of the Swis client. This is replaced by the new BufferingLogger class (a Psr\Log\AbstractLogger decorator) that wraps any injected logger, retains the last ten log records in memory, and exposes getStderrErrors() to extract lines the SDK's StdioTransport logs under the message "Server stderr". The BufferingLogger instance is created inside initializeClient() and passed to both the transport and the SDK Client::builder(), so stderr is captured without any reflection.

listTools() now follows the MCP pagination cursor in a bounded loop (capped at MAX_TOOL_PAGES = 100), treating a null or empty-string cursor as terminal. An empty JSON object that the SDK preserves as \stdClass (for example an inputSchema['properties'] of a parameterless tool) is normalized to a plain PHP array via a json_encode/json_decode round-trip in normalizeToArray(). executeTool() uses an instanceof TextContent check rather than method_exists() probing; other content types go through jsonSerialize() and the same normalization. McpToolBase::doExecute() is updated to look up tools via the ToolCollection API ($server->getTool($tool_name)) rather than the removed name-keyed tools array and the separate enabled_tools property. The STDIO transport environment variable handling now merges configured variables over the current parent environment (via array_merge(getenv(), $config['env'])) rather than replacing it, preserving PATH and other inherited variables.

Upgrade

The public class Drupal\mcp_client\Transporter\CustomHeadersHttpTransporter has been removed. Any code that extended or directly instantiated it must be updated.

Before:

use Drupal\mcp_client\Transporter\CustomHeadersHttpTransporter;
// ...
$transporter = new CustomHeadersHttpTransporter($endpoint, $headers, $logger);

After: custom HTTP headers are passed directly through MCPClient's configuration array via McpClientFactory, and the transport is created internally. There is no supported replacement class to extend; inject headers through the module's server configuration instead.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 9, 2026
  • Committed: July 20, 2026 (10 days later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

Collaboration

guignonv asked in the thread whether it was worth continuing to contribute bug reports against the Swis library given the pending migration. marcus_johansson confirmed the AI Initiative had already picked up this issue in an active sprint and that the migration would land within two weeks, which it did.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588043: Add drupal-gitlab skill: GitLab CLI guidance tailored to git.drupalcode.org
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +366/−0 · md +366
  • Discussion: #3588043 · 3 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The ai_best_practices module gains a new drupal-gitlab skill that gives AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) precise, actionable guidance for GitLab CLI operations on git.drupalcode.org. The skill covers authentication with glab, Drupal's issue-fork workflow, Conventional Commits format with By: attribution, cross-project merge request creation via the REST API, work item management via the GraphQL API, CI/CD pipeline monitoring, and the required AI-disclosure field in MR descriptions. A companion README documents glab installation and initial host configuration for human contributors.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New drupal-gitlab skill added at skills/drupal-gitlab/SKILL.md and skills/drupal-gitlab/README.md for AI agent GitLab CLI workflows on git.drupalcode.org

Technical details

The MR adds two files under skills/drupal-gitlab/: SKILL.md (the agent-facing instruction file) and README.md (the human-facing setup guide).

SKILL.md addresses several Drupal-specific constraints that vanilla glab guidance does not cover. Cross-project MRs (from an issue fork to the upstream project) are not supported by glab mr create, so the skill instructs agents to use the GitLab REST API directly, fetching both the source (issue/<project>-<issue-id>) and target (project/<repo>) project IDs before constructing the payload. Work item status is a widget, not a label, so reading or updating it requires a GraphQL workItemUpdate mutation rather than a label command. Issue forks cannot be created by pushing or via the API because Drupal's CDN intercepts those routes and returns a 301 to drupal.org/git-error; the skill instructs the agent to direct the user to the Drupal.org management page (https://new.drupal.org/drupalorg/issue-fork/management?source_link=...) instead. The GITLAB_HOST environment variable must be passed to every glab command, and --hostname must additionally be passed to glab api and glab auth subcommands. Commit messages must follow Drupal's Conventional Commits format including By: lines with Drupal.org usernames.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 1, 2026

Collaboration

During review, breidert documented a significant infrastructure constraint discovered while testing: the issue fork cannot be created programmatically via API or push because Drupal's CDN intercepts those requests and returns a 301 redirect to drupal.org/git-error. He updated both files to make the required manual provisioning step explicit and to note that the workflow can be updated once Drupal's infrastructure supports programmatic fork creation.

marcus_johansson stress-tested the skill by using it to create a parent issue and 16 linked sub-issues in the AI module's queue in a single Claude Code session. He noted that the agent correctly followed issue templates, added sensible labels, and used a Python script to batch-write sub-issues efficiently, while also flagging one gap: the skill did not discourage creating GitLab subtasks (which cannot receive contribution credit) versus standalone issues.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601377: Support conditional (depends_on) custom questions in .drupalaibp.json
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal unknown (0)
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601377 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer project's .drupalaibp.json config format now supports conditional questions. A question entry can include a depends_on field that gates the prompt on another question's answer. If the condition is not met the question is silently skipped, its answer is set to an empty string in .ddev/.env, and the user is never shown the prompt. This is useful for things like "only ask for a Redis TTL when the user opted into Redis" rather than always asking both questions regardless of context.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: Any depends_on in a config's questions block disables .ddev/.env answer reuse for the entire block on every re-run.
  • New API: New depends_on field in .drupalaibp.json question entries, supporting equals, not_equals, and in condition kinds against an earlier question's answer.

Technical details

The implementation adds _config_read_depends_on to src/lib/config/load_config.sh. It runs for every question entry in _config_read_questions and populates three new parallel arrays: CONFIG_QUESTION_DEP_ENV, CONFIG_QUESTION_DEP_KIND, and CONFIG_QUESTION_DEP_VALUES. Every question gets exactly one entry in these arrays (empty strings when no depends_on is present), keeping them parallel to CONFIG_QUESTION_KEYS.

Three condition kinds are supported: equals (string equality), not_equals (string inequality), and in (the dependency's answer is one of an array of values; stored internally as a comma-joined string). The depends_on.env value must reference a question that appears earlier in the same questions array. A forward reference, self-reference, a name that matches no earlier question, or a depends_on block that lacks all three condition keys triggers a warning at load time and falls back to asking the question unconditionally rather than silently dropping it.

ask_config_questions in src/lib/shared/prompts/ask_config_questions.sh evaluates the condition against ANSWERS[dep_env] before prompting. A failing condition sets ANSWERS[key]="" and skips the prompt entirely, even in unattended runs where the question's own default would otherwise be used. The value pre-answer field short-circuits before depends_on is evaluated, so a pre-answered question is never conditionally skipped.

A boolean flag CONFIG_HAS_CONDITIONAL_QUESTION is set by _config_read_depends_on as soon as any valid depends_on is parsed. ask_config_questions uses this flag to disable .ddev/.env reuse for the entire questions block, not just the conditional questions. The rationale documented in AGENTS.md and docs/drupalaibp-json.md is that a stale saved value for a dependency question could silently gate a dependent question using an answer the user never confirmed in the current run. The cost is that every question in the block, including unconditional and password-type ones, is re-prompted on every re-run of that config.

Upgrade

Config authors add depends_on to any question entry:

{
  "questions": [
    { "env": "USE_REDIS", "label": "Use Redis?", "type": "text", "default": "no" },
    {
      "env": "REDIS_TTL",
      "label": "Redis cache TTL (seconds)",
      "type": "text",
      "default": "3600",
      "depends_on": { "env": "USE_REDIS", "equals": "yes" }
    }
  ]
}

Other condition kinds:

"depends_on": { "env": "MODE", "not_equals": "prod" }
"depends_on": { "env": "FLAVOR", "in": ["blog", "shop"] }

depends_on.env must name a question that appears earlier in the array. A forward reference or a missing condition key (equals/not_equals/in) produces a warning and the question is asked unconditionally. Adding any depends_on to a config disables .ddev/.env answer reuse for the whole questions block on every re-run.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 17, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601357: GitHub: default to private repo and prompt for public
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601357 · 1 contributor · 10 comments

Summary

When the one_line_installer prompts you to create a GitHub repository for a new site, it now defaults to private visibility. Previously the prompt defaulted to public, meaning a new site could be published to GitHub publicly unless the user explicitly chose otherwise. Now you must opt in to make the repo public.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The change affects three places in src/. The global initializer in src/setup_site_command.sh now sets GH_VISIBILITY="--private" as the starting default, with a comment noting that repos are created private unless the user explicitly opts into public. In src/lib/steps/github_setup.sh, the github_setup function's visibility prompt now lists "Private" as the first option (index 0) with MENU_DEFAULT=0 pre-highlighting it, and "Public" as the second option; selecting index 0 sets GH_VISIBILITY="--private", index 1 sets --public. The --github-setup CLI flag path in src/lib/shared/prompts/apply_cli_flags.sh also sets GH_VISIBILITY="--private" (it had no visibility override before; the non-interactive flag path now hard-codes private, consistent with the prompt default).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 3, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601375: Post-install hand-off: open the project shell on inactivity instead of on Enter
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal task
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601375 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The post-install shell hand-off in the one_line_installer now opens the project shell by default on inactivity instead of requiring an explicit keypress. Previously, the 30-second countdown required the user to press Enter to open a shell in the new project directory, and timing out simply printed the directory path. The prompt is now inverted: the shell opens automatically after 30 seconds of inactivity, and pressing Enter is what skips it. This eliminates an extra keypress for the common case where a human just finished an install and wants to start working immediately. Automated or agent-driven runs that reach this prompt without --yolo will now drop into an interactive shell rather than timing out safely; such callers should pass --yolo, which bypasses the prompt entirely.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The change is in src/lib/steps/agent_handoff.sh inside step_agent_handoff. The read -t 30 call and the branch logic are inverted: read -r -t 30 now returns zero on Enter (skip) and non-zero on timeout (open shell). The timeout path falls through to exec "${SHELL:-bash}" </dev/tty, and the Enter path returns after printing the cd hint. The prompt copy changed from "Press Enter within 30s to open a shell…" to "Opening a shell in your project in 30s — press Enter to skip…". The guard for --yolo and non-tty environments (YOLO=1 or /dev/tty unavailable) is unchanged: those paths print the project path and return without any countdown. The agent_handoff.bats test assertion was updated to check for "press Enter to skip" absence under --yolo, confirming the new prompt text. The generated drupalaibp script must be regenerated after this src/ change, per the project's standard workflow.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 15, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601376: Allow .drupalaibp.json to append or replace the welcome text
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601376 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

Projects shipping a .drupalaibp.json config can now customize the "you're all set" summary that the one-line installer prints at the end of a successful build. Two optional keys are available: append_welcome_text adds extra paragraphs after the standard summary (site URL, admin login, handy commands), and replace_welcome_text replaces the entire summary with custom content. Both keys work for Drupal and non-Drupal builds. When both are set, replace_welcome_text wins and append_welcome_text is ignored. The feature is documented in docs/drupalaibp-json.md including a full token and color reference.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Two new .drupalaibp.json keys: append_welcome_text and replace_welcome_text, with {TOKEN} and color-code substitution in both.

Technical details

Both keys are read by the new _config_read_welcome_text function in src/lib/config/load_config.sh into APPEND_WELCOME_TEXT and REPLACE_WELCOME_TEXT bash arrays. Each key accepts either a bare string or an array of strings (paragraphs); the reader handles both shapes.

print_welcome in src/lib/steps/print_welcome.sh checks REPLACE_WELCOME_TEXT first: when the array is non-empty it iterates and prints each paragraph through _welcome_text_substitute, then returns before any of the standard summary is built. If only APPEND_WELCOME_TEXT is set, the standard summary prints in full and the extra paragraphs are appended at the end via the same helper.

The new helper src/lib/steps/_welcome_text_substitute.sh performs {TOKEN} substitution. Built-in tokens are {PROJECT_NAME}, {SITE_URL}, {DRUPAL_USERNAME}, {DRUPAL_PASSWORD}, and {DRUPAL_LOGIN_URL}. Any key from the config's questions array is also available by its env name. Color tokens ({BOLD}, {DIM}, {GREEN}, {YELLOW}, {RED}, {RESET}) expand to ANSI codes only when stdout is a real TTY (gated by _welcome_text_colors_enabled which wraps [[ -t 1 ]]); they collapse to nothing in piped or redirected output. An unrecognized token is left untouched rather than silently deleted, surfacing typos in the terminal output. Every paragraph receives an automatic trailing RESET when colors are enabled so an unclosed color token cannot bleed into subsequent output. Both new keys are registered in DRUPALAIBP_KNOWN_KEYS so the unknown-key warning does not fire on valid configs.

Upgrade

No changes required for existing configs; both keys are optional and the default behavior (standard summary, no appended or replaced text) is unchanged.

To append project-specific instructions after the standard summary:

{
  "append_welcome_text": [
    "Project docs: https://example.com/handbook",
    "Ask in #project-support if you get stuck."
  ]
}

To replace the entire summary with custom content (accepts a bare string or array):

{
  "replace_welcome_text": "{BOLD}{GREEN}Welcome to the ACME demo!{RESET} Run `ddev launch` and log in with admin / admin."
}

Tokens from questions[].env answers are available in both keys, e.g. {SITE_FLAVOR} when a question with "env": "SITE_FLAVOR" is defined. Unknown tokens are left verbatim in the output rather than silently removed.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 16, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601363: Add --config-path to install from a local, HTTPS, or repo-hosted .drupalaibp.json
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601363 · 1 contributor · 4 comments

Summary

The setup-site command gains a --config-path <dir> flag, a third way to point the installer at a .drupalaibp.json alongside the existing --global-config (a named file in ~/.drupalaibp/configs/) and --config-url (a git-cloned repo). Passing a local directory path — such as a folder checked into the current project — loads its root .drupalaibp.json, drives the build from it, and resolves sibling hook scripts and copy_paths assets relative to that directory. This lets teams share a config by committing it to their repo and passing its directory at build time, without copying the file into each developer's global config store. The flag is local-only and intentionally has no network or URL support; for remote configs, --config-url is the appropriate tool.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New --config-path <dir> flag on setup-site builds from a .drupalaibp.json in a local directory, mutually exclusive with --config-url and --global-config.

Technical details

The implementation lives in two new files: src/lib/config/_resolve_config_path_source.sh (_resolve_config_path_source) validates that the argument is an existing directory containing a .drupalaibp.json, then sets RESOLVED_CONFIG_SOURCE to its absolute path. src/lib/config/load_config_path.sh (load_config_path) calls that resolver and then hands the resolved path to the existing drupalaibp_load machinery, which populates all choice globals and registers config hooks exactly as it does for other load paths. setup_site_command.sh dispatches to load_config_path when --config-path is present and PROJECT_CONTEXT == none.

Compared to --global-config, --config-path sets only USE_CONFIG=1, not YOLO=1: the config's own questions still prompt interactively unless --yolo is passed separately. CONFIG_BASEDIR is set to the given directory (via the normal drupalaibp_load call), so hooks[].script and copy_paths entries resolve relative to it and are copied into the project by the existing _copy_hook_scripts/_copy_config_paths machinery. No key restriction, trust gate, or HTTPS fetch is involved; those concerns belong to --config-url.

--config-path is mutually exclusive with --config-url and --global-config; passing more than one of the three causes an immediate die. The flag is silently rejected in management mode (it only applies when PROJECT_CONTEXT == none). The bats suite covers the happy path, CONFIG_BASEDIR positioning, a missing .drupalaibp.json, and a nonexistent directory in tests/bats/lib/config/load_config_path.bats and _resolve_config_path_source.bats.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 9, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601371: Make the build script only move the main script on tags
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601371 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

The one-line installer's GitLab Pages publish step now fires only when a Git tag is pushed. Before this change the CI pages job ran on every merge to the default branch, meaning the public URL https://project.pages.drupalcode.org/one_line_installer/drupalaibp could serve unreleased, in-progress work to anyone running the one-liner. After this change that URL always reflects the most recent tagged release. The workflow for maintainers is now: merge the change, then tag the commit to publish it.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The pages job in .gitlab-ci.yml gained a rules: - if: '$CI_COMMIT_TAG' guard. The job builds the MkDocs docs site and copies drupalaibp alongside it (cp drupalaibp public/drupalaibp), then publishes the whole public/ directory to GitLab Pages.

The entire job is tag-gated rather than just the cp line because a Pages deploy replaces the whole site: a docs-only deploy from the default branch would publish a public/ directory without drupalaibp in it, deleting the installer from the live URL until the next tag. Keeping docs and script together and shipping both only on a tag avoids that window.

The bashly_verify job still runs on every pipeline (merge or tag), so drift between src/ and the committed drupalaibp is caught continuously. The tag pipeline runs bashly_verify as a prerequisite for pages (via needs), so a drifted script cannot be published even if a tag is pushed without regenerating.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 14, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601370: Agent Ready Drupal Build Kit
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601370 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer now offers the Agent-Ready Drupal Build Kit as an optional extra during setup. When selected, the installer adds a pre-packaged agent skill to every coding agent the user chose (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode), giving the agent a defined workflow for content modelling, verification, and review-packet generation when rebuilding an existing website as a Drupal CMS site. The extra is opt-in (not installed by default) and requires the Drupal CMS profile; choosing it on a standard or minimal profile skips installation and prints a prominent warning.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New agent_ready_drupal_build_kit installer extra: selectable via --extras agent_ready_drupal_build_kit or the interactive menu; requires the Drupal CMS profile (cms or template)

Technical details

The extra is delivered as src/lib/extras/extra_agent_ready_drupal_build_kit.sh. The installer discovers extras at runtime by scanning the function namespace for extra_<id>_meta symbols (see _extras_discover.sh), so no prompt, global, or pipeline change was needed beyond dropping in the new file.

Unlike the other extras (dtk, droost, tool_api), this one installs no Drupal module. It calls npx --yes skills add 'https://github.com/scottfalconer/agent-ready-drupal-build-kit' inside the DDEV web container, iterating over _skill_agent_targets to register the skill for each chosen agent. After installation it appends a note to the managed block in AGENTS.md.

The extra_agent_ready_drupal_build_kit_meta() function omits the default flag so the extra starts unticked in the interactive menu and is not installed under --yolo / unattended mode. A profile check gates execution: PROFILE must be cms or template (both scaffold drupal/cms); any other profile causes _build_kit_skipped_banner to print a bold-red ASCII block and returns early. The extra can also be selected non-interactively with --extras agent_ready_drupal_build_kit or added on top of the defaults with --add-extras agent_ready_drupal_build_kit.

Upgrade

No upgrade steps needed for existing sites. To use the new extra on a fresh install:

# Interactive menu — the extra appears as an opt-in choice
bash <(curl -fsSL https://project.pages.drupalcode.org/one_line_installer/drupalaibp)

# Or select it non-interactively (Drupal CMS profile is required)
drupalaibp --profile cms --extras agent_ready_drupal_build_kit

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 14, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601332: Adopt Bashly (bashly.dev) to generate the installer from a single YAML-defined CLI
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major task
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601332 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer project adopted Bashly as its build framework, replacing roughly 750 lines of hand-written bash spread across four files with a single generated script called drupalaibp. The committed drupalaibp file (around 4,900 lines) is generated from a src/bashly.yml CLI definition plus per-command and shared-library partials in src/lib/. End users run the same bash <(curl …) invocation; they now target the drupalaibp script rather than the old install.sh. Maintainers who regenerate the script need the bashly Ruby gem (version pinned in .bashly-version). A CI job (bashly_verify) fails if the committed script ever drifts from src/.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New .drupalaibp.json config schema for unattended, config-driven builds with provisioning, custom questions, and hook scripts at named pipeline events.

Technical details

The old installer duplicated step/ok/warn/die helpers and ANSI color constants across install.sh, install-macos.sh, install-linux.sh, and github-push.sh. Bashly solves this by injecting a shared library into the generated bundle. Each shared helper now lives once: src/lib/shared/colors.sh, src/lib/shared/output_helpers.sh, src/lib/shared/select_one.sh, src/lib/shared/select_many.sh, and so on.

The CLI is structured as three subcommands defined in src/bashly.yml: setup-site (the default, covering fresh installs and existing-project detection), install (registers the tool as a global drupal-setup command in ~/.local/bin), and delete (removes a DDEV project). Automatic --help, --version, and flag validation come from Bashly's generated argument parser, which requires bash 4. A src/lib/core/00_require_bash.sh shim re-execs under a newer bash when macOS ships bash 3.2.

The Bashly adoption also carried a significant feature expansion. A .drupalaibp.json config format was added (src/lib/config/load_config.sh, src/lib/config/hooks.sh) that lets maintainers drive unattended builds from a JSON file declaring profile, coding agents, skill repos, Composer packages, modules, recipes, DDEV add-ons, custom interactive questions, and hook scripts fired at named pipeline events (ddev_started, coding_agent_added, etc.). The config-export path (drupalaibp_export) serializes the interactive choices back to JSON in pure bash without a jq runtime dependency. The --config-url flag clones an external git repo containing a .drupalaibp.json and uses it as the build config.

Upgrade

Users previously invoking the old install.sh entry point should switch to drupalaibp:

# Old (1.0.x)
bash <(curl -fsSL https://git.drupalcode.org/project/one_line_installer/-/raw/1.0.x/install.sh)

# New (2.0.x)
bash <(curl -fsSL https://project.pages.drupalcode.org/one_line_installer/drupalaibp)

Maintainers who need to regenerate the committed script require the Bashly gem at the pinned version:

gem install bashly -v "$(cat .bashly-version)"
bin/generate   # regenerates drupalaibp from src/

The -yolo flag from 1.0.x is now --yolo (double-dash). Config-driven builds use a .drupalaibp.json placed in the launch directory; see docs/config.md and docs/examples/ for the full schema.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 1, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • unqunq (DigitalTrotter)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601367: Fix the viewport issue
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601367 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer's interactive menus (select_one for single-choice and select_many for checkbox selections) would corrupt the terminal display whenever the option list was taller than the terminal window. Each keypress would shove the entire display further up the screen instead of moving just the cursor. This is now fixed: menus clamp themselves to a scrollable viewport sized to the terminal height, showing only a window of options at a time with "↑ N more" / "↓ N more" indicators when the list is clipped. A related wrapping bug is also fixed: labels longer than the terminal width would wrap onto a second physical line, throwing off the repaint's cursor-up jump; labels are now truncated with an ellipsis and terminal line-wrap is disabled for the menu's duration.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Both menus repaint by issuing \033[<block>A to jump the cursor back up over the block they last drew, counting option rows. When the full option list exceeds the terminal height, printing the last row scrolls the buffer: the list no longer starts <block> rows above the cursor, so the jump lands on the wrong row and each subsequent repaint pushes the window further up. The fix is in src/lib/core/_menu_viewport.sh, which introduces five helpers used by both selectors.

_menu_term_height and _menu_term_width read terminal dimensions via stty size 2>/dev/null </dev/tty, falling back to 24 and 80 when there is no terminal. _menu_viewport <n> computes three globals: MENU_VIEW (option rows to paint), MENU_CLIPPED (1 when the list is clipped), and MENU_OFFSET (index of the first visible option). Available rows are terminal_height - MENU_CHROME_LINES where MENU_CHROME_LINES=3 accounts for the prompt, the key-hint line, and one spare row so the block never ends on the very last screen line. When clipped, MENU_VIEW is reduced by 2 to leave room for the indicator lines, which are part of the counted block. _menu_scroll <cur> <n> is called before every repaint and slides MENU_OFFSET so the highlighted row stays inside [MENU_OFFSET, MENU_OFFSET + MENU_VIEW). _menu_more_line draws the "↑/↓ N more" line, printing a blank-but-cleared line when nothing is hidden in that direction, so the block height remains constant and the cursor-up jump stays exact.

The second bug — a label wider than the terminal wrapping onto a second physical row — is addressed by _menu_fit <text> <reserve>, which truncates the label to _menu_term_width minus the prefix width and appends …. Both select_one and select_many additionally disable terminal line-wrap with \033[?7l for the duration of the menu and restore it with \033[?7h on exit, as a second safeguard. The skills menu was the concrete trigger: its option labels ran up to 111 columns on an 80-column terminal.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 14, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601369: Add the possibility to add one skill or extra to the default
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601369 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer tool now supports --add-skills and --add-extras flags that extend the default agent skills and extra Drupal installations rather than replacing them. Previously, using --skills or --extras required listing every value you wanted, which would discard the defaults. The new additive flags let you say "give me the defaults plus these specific additions" without having to repeat the full default list. Both flags also compose with a loaded config: --add-skills extends a config's skills list, and --add-extras extends a config's extras rather than overwriting them.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New --add-skills and --add-extras CLI flags added to setup-site; they extend defaults (or a loaded config) rather than replacing them.

Technical details

Two new flags are declared in src/bashly.yml and processed in apply_cli_flags() inside src/lib/shared/prompts/apply_cli_flags.sh.

--add-skills accepts a comma-separated list of 'repo|skill' specifiers. When applied, it reads the default skill specs via _default_skill_specs() (or the config's SKILLS_SPEC array when USE_CONFIG=1), normalizes each added entry through _skill_spec_normalize(), then merges them with the base list using the new _merge_added() helper. The result is stored back in SKILLS_SPEC and SKILLS_SET=1 is set, which causes choose_skills to skip the interactive prompt.

--add-extras accepts a comma-separated list of extra IDs. It selects the base from whichever is already set: if --extras was also passed (or a config is active), the existing EXTRAS array is the base; otherwise _extras_default_ids() supplies the defaults. _merge_added() appends the new entries, deduplicating against the base in insertion order, and stores the result in EXTRAS with EXTRAS_SET=1.

_merge_added() is a small helper that takes a CSV string plus a list of base items, walks both in order, and prints each unique item exactly once. It handles skill specs that contain | and * without word-splitting or glob expansion by never passing them through bare variable expansion.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 14, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601361: Replace the experimental tool prompts with a generic, script-driven "Extra Drupal Installations" multi-select
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major unknown (0)
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601361 · 1 contributor · 5 comments

Summary

The one-line installer now offers all optional Drupal tools through a single "Any extra Drupal installations?" multi-select prompt instead of separate yes/no questions for each tool. Three extras ship today: Drush Token Killer (dtk, pre-ticked and installed by default under --yolo), Tool API + Toolbelt (tool_api, opt-in), and the new Droost AI-agent MCP integration (droost, opt-in). Anyone using the old --tool-api flag must switch to --extras tool_api. Config files should use the new extra_scripts array key instead of per-tool booleans. Adding a new optional tool no longer requires editing prompts, globals, the pipeline, or config readers: dropping one file into src/lib/extras/ and regenerating is all that is needed.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: .drupalaibp.json gains extra_scripts (array of ids: dtk, tool_api, droost); the old per-tool boolean keys are no longer read.
  • API change: The --tool-api boolean flag is removed; use --extras tool_api (or a comma-separated list) instead.
  • New API: New extra_installed <id> event emitted after each successful extra install; new src/lib/extras/ convention for adding optional tools as extra_<id>_meta + extra_<id> function pairs.

Technical details

The old architecture had a dedicated prompt function (choose_toolbelt, choose_dtk, choose_tool_api), a per-tool global (TOOL_API, TOOLBELT, DTK), a per-tool install step (step_install_tool_api), and a per-tool boolean CLI flag (--tool-api) for every optional install. Each addition required changes across at least five places.

The new architecture introduces src/lib/extras/ as a convention. Each extra defines exactly two bash functions: extra_<id>_meta (prints Name|Description, or Name|Description|default to mark it as a default) and extra_<id> (performs the install using step/ok/warn and ddev/drush). The single _extras_discover function finds them at runtime via compgen -A function scanning the bash function namespace, because the entire script is bundled by Bashly and extras cannot be globbed off disk. choose_extras (registered on ask_questions at weight 45) builds one select_many menu from the discovered extras, pre-ticking defaults via MENU_PRESELECT, and sets the EXTRAS array of ids. The generic step_install_extras (pipeline slot 10, replacing step_install_tool_api) iterates EXTRAS, calls extra_<id>, and emits extra_installed <id> on success. Failures warn and continue.

The --tool-api boolean flag is replaced by --extras LIST (comma-separated ids). Presence of the flag, not its value, overrides the defaults: --extras "" is a valid explicit "install nothing." The .drupalaibp.json config schema gains extra_scripts (array of ids), read into EXTRAS by load_config.sh. Old per-tool boolean keys (toolbelt, dtk) are not read; the project documents this as part of its 2.0 schema clean break. export_config.sh writes extra_scripts with the selected ids rather than expanding packages into composer_projects/install_modules, so configs no longer need to track package names separately from the extras themselves. A new extra_installed event joins the event registry alongside ddev_addon_added and skill_added.

Also in this commit: the Bashly generator version is pinned to a .bashly-version file and a bin/generate wrapper enforces the pin, preventing CI failures caused by version drift between local and CI generators.

Upgrade

If you use the CLI flag, replace --tool-api with --extras tool_api:

# Before
drupalaibp --tool-api

# After
drupalaibp --extras tool_api
# Multiple extras (comma-separated)
drupalaibp --extras tool_api,droost
# Explicit "none" (overrides the dtk default)
drupalaibp --extras ""

If you have a .drupalaibp.json config that listed Tool API packages manually, replace those entries with the extra_scripts key:

// Before (manual package listing)
{
  "composer_projects": ["drupal/tool", "drupal/tool_belt"],
  "install_modules": ["tool", "tool_belt", "tool_belt_content", "..."],
  "composer_allow_plugins": ["tbachert/spi"]
}

// After
{
  "extra_scripts": ["tool_api"]
}

To add a new optional tool, create one file in src/lib/extras/ following this pattern, then run bin/generate:

# src/lib/extras/extra_mymodule.sh
extra_mymodule_meta() {
  printf '%s|%s\n' "My Module" "short description shown in the menu"
  # Add |default as a third field to pre-tick it
}

extra_mymodule() {
  ddev exec composer require drupal/mymodule --no-interaction || { warn "Could not require mymodule."; return 1; }
  ddev drush en -y mymodule || warn "Could not enable mymodule."
  ok "My Module installed."
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 9, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
